prestazioni: 4G63T (7-bolt) engine swap from a 1995-1999 Mitsubishi Eclipse GSX, retaining the FWD layout but upgrading to a Garrett GT3076R turbocharger with a 0.82 A/R turbine housing and 56 trim compressor wheel for 350-400whp on E85 fuel, paired with a Tomei 272° camshaft set and Manley stainless steel valves

Full bolt-on supporting mods including a GReddy Type-S intercooler (2.5" core, 24"x12"x3.5"), AEM 3.5" intake with velocity stack, and a custom 3" turbo-back exhaust featuring a Burns Stainless 4-2-1 header and a MagnaFlow 14815 cat-back system with 3.5" dual outlets

Chassis reinforcement via a Cusco front strut tower bar (2-point, 22mm diameter) and a rear subframe brace (Mitsubishi Motorsport part #MZ312992), combined with BC Racing BR coilovers (500lb/in front, 400lb/in rear) and Whiteline 24mm adjustable rear sway bar (part #BMR79XZ)
estetica: Rocket Bunny V1 widebody kit (Pandem) with custom-fabricated front splitter and rear diffuser, finished in BASF RM+ matte white with gloss black accents, including extended side skirts and over-fenders for 275/35/18 Nitto NT05 tires on Work Emotion CR Kiwami wheels (18x9.5 +22 front, 18x10.5 +30 rear)

Custom convertible top delete with a SpeedHut carbon fiber hardtop (part #SH-001) featuring a rear spoiler from a 2003-2005 Mitsubishi Eclipse GT (OEM part #MR497632) and a Seibon carbon fiber hood (part #MTE-CF-HOOD-001) with functional vents

Full OEM+ interior refresh with Recaro SRD-LC seats (part #SRD-LC-F) in black Alcantara with red stitching, a NRG Innovations quick-release steering wheel hub (part #NRG-SK-201B) paired with a Momo Mod.78 steering wheel, and a custom roll bar (Autopower part #70449) wrapped in red suede
